sentence_reviews.text: Sampling, alternating latent-variable estimation, and local factor updates approximate difficult inference problems.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing is to connect Bayesian & approximate inference, Graph theory to downstream capabilities including k-means, mixture models & clustering, Hidden Markov models & CRFs, Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs.

sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports EM, clustering, mixture models, and graphical models, but the inspected evidence does not directly show HMMs/CRFs or the full dependency chain.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Bayesian & approximate inference, Graph theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML table of contents lines 116-119 list empirical risk minimization and surrogate loss; lines 101-104 list graphical models; lines 131-139 list Bayesian statistics and computational issues.
sentence_reviews.notes: The surrogate-loss and dependency ingredients are supported, but the behavioral-failure warning is not directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Bayesian & approximate inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML table of contents lines 71, 101-104, 131-139, and 293-296 list Monte Carlo approximation, probabilistic graphical model inference, Bayesian machine learning, computational issues, and EM.
sentence_reviews.notes: These methods are approximate inference methods; the 'learned, selected, or evaluated' wording is less direct than a simple uses relation.

sentence_reviews.text: MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing uses Graph theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML table of contents lines 101-104 list probabilistic graphical model representation, inference, and learning.
sentence_reviews.notes: Message passing in probabilistic graphical models directly rests on graph-structured representations.

sentence_reviews.text: MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML table of contents lines 260-296 list optimization methods, bound optimization, and the EM algorithm.
sentence_reviews.notes: EM is directly optimization-related; MCMC and message passing are broader inference procedures, so common strength is more defensible than a universal core dependency.

edge_reviews.notes: MCMC, EM, and message passing are approximate/probabilistic inference techniques; core strength is defensible.
Probabilistic graphical model representation and inference directly support a graph-theory dependency for message passing; core strength is supported for this grouped node.
EM is presented under bound optimization; optimization is a common but not universal dependency across the grouped methods.
